w:r.w:t: Can you make a Minecraft Bedrock addon?
Yes. I can help create a Minecraft Bedrock add-on, including:
Scripts using the Bedrock Script API
Complete behavior packs and resource packs
A new ore that drops custom gems
A dragon boss with custom attacks
New biomes and structures (using Bedrock-compatible methods)
Which Minecraft Bedrock version you're targeting.
Whether you want scripting (JavaScript) or a no-script add-on.
Whether you want the actual file structure/code or just design help.
If you describe the add-on idea, I can generate the complete folder structure and all the JSON/JavaScript files needed.
Goal: Additional uses for pigs, eggs, and other breakfast and breakfast adjacent items.
Blocks: Griddle - behaves like the campfire, but is a griddle with a flat cooktop. Recipe: top row - iron ingots | into ingot | iron ingots
Middle row - wood plank | empty | wood plank
Bottom row - wood plank | empty | wood plank
Butcher block: uses knife to process meats and other food items. Behaves similarly to Farmer's Delight cutting board. Steak into strips, pork chop into bacon or ham, etc.
Items: knife. Similar to Farmer's Delight knife.
Bacon, ham, sausage, raw egg (on griddle), fried egg
Dish Vanilla Ingredients, Nutrition / Saturation, Unique Mechanic / Effect
Fried Egg Egg (Smoker / Campfire) 3 / 0.4 Ultra-fast consumption time.
Toast with Jam Wheat + Sweet Berries / Glow Berries 5 / 0.6 Glow berry variant grants temporary Glow effect.
Berry Pancakes Wheat + Milk + Egg + Sweet Berries 8 / 0.8 Grants Haste I for 60 seconds (morning energy boost).
The Miner's Skillet Porkchop (Bacon) + Potato + Fried Egg 12 / 1.2 Clears Mining Fatigue instantly when eaten.
Nether Fungi Omelet Egg + Crimson Fungus + Warped Fungus 6 / 0.6 Grants 15 seconds of Fire Resistance, with a short side of Nausea.
Omelet - egg + meat + veggie / mushroom, 5 3:00 regen
That's a solid Bedrock add-on concept. Everything you've listed is feasible in Bedrock Edition, though a few mechanics depend on whether you're willing to use the Script API.
Expand breakfast foods and early-game cooking with:
More uses for pigs, eggs, berries, potatoes, and fungi
Doesn't visually display food on top
Acts almost identically to Farmer's Delight cutting board.
Uses food effect removal event in Script API.
Can instead grant Haste I for 90 seconds.
Pigs become a valuable food source.
A feature I'd strongly recommend adding is Hash Browns (potato + knife on butcher block, then griddle), because they fit the breakfast theme and give potatoes another useful food progression.

One thing to note: Mojang changed Minecraft's version numbering in 2026. Bedrock versions are now using the new year-based numbering system (26.x), replacing the older 1.21.x progression. The first 2026 game drops began using the new format, so targeting the latest 26.x release is the right approach for a new add-on.
For the Breakfast project, I recommend we establish:
Minecraft Bedrock Edition: Latest 26.x stable release
Realms: Goal for compatibility where possible
Easy upgrade path from v1.0 → v1.5

Some Bedrock APIs and JSON schemas changed during the 1.21 → 26.x transition, so every generated file should be written specifically for the latest stable Bedrock Creator documentation rather than older 1.20/1.21 examples.
I'll prepare a complete registry with:
Before I generate Operation 1, I have one design question that will affect nearly every recipe:
Should Breakfast use vanilla Bedrock crafting tables for most recipes, or should the Griddle act as the primary cooking workstation with its own custom cooking system (closer to Farmer's Delight)?
Crafting Table = simple foods (jam, biscuits, sandwiches)
That creates a clear progression and gives the Griddle a meaningful role.
Excellent. That gives us a solid design philosophy for Breakfast v1.0.
Players shouldn't go directly from animal → meal.
Consume a Nether Fungi Omelet in the Nether.
I see three things worth deciding before Operation 2:
Or function like a campfire once placed?
My recommendation: fuel-free for v1.0 to keep the cooking loop simple.
Once those are settled, we'll lock Operation 1 and move into Operation 2: Behavior Pack Foundation for Bedrock 26.x.
Messages beyond this point are only visible to you
Perfect. Operation 1 is now locked for Breakfast v1.0.
Functions as a dedicated cooking workstation
Designed as a convenience upgrade over campfires
These are intentionally not part of v1.0:
Before I generate Operation 2, I have one final architectural question:
A custom interactive block with its own inventory UI (scripted)
More likely to break with API changes
A visually unique block that uses right-click interactions and scripts without opening a custom UI
For Bedrock 26.x, I recommend Option B for v1.0. It will be significantly easier to maintain and test, while still delivering the gameplay loop you want.
